evals.prompt: It's the end of the day and I want to wrap up NemoClaw cleanly — check where we landed against today's version target, make sure QA has what they need, and ship a tag if it makes sense. Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: Walk the user through the end-of-day handoff: determine the target version, show shipped vs open progress, bump stragglers to the next patch, generate a QA handoff summary, then delegate to cut-release-tag for the actual tag, and finally record state.
assertions: Response determines the current version target before making any changes (for example by running version-target.ts or equivalent progress check)
Response presents shipped vs open progress to the user before cutting anything
Response describes generating a QA handoff summary covering commits since the last tag and risky areas
Response delegates the actual tagging to the cut-release-tag skill rather than reinventing that workflow inline
Response explicitly asks for user confirmation before cutting the tag
Response does NOT skip straight to creating a new semver tag without checking progress first

evals.prompt: Wrapping up for the night. A few of today's labeled items didn't land — one is still waiting on CI and another has review comments outstanding. What do I do with them before I cut the tag?
evals.expected_output: Recognize the stragglers case: bump the unshipped items to the next patch version label so they are not lost, then proceed with the handoff summary and tag for whatever actually shipped. If nothing shipped, offer to skip the tag.
assertions: Response identifies the unshipped items as stragglers that should be moved to the next version label rather than left on the current one
Response references bumping stragglers via the bump-stragglers script or describes moving the version label forward for the open items
Response explains that the version label follows the item to the next patch version (labels are living markers, not historical records)
Response still produces a handoff summary and tag for the items that did ship today
Response mentions that if nothing shipped the tag can be skipped for the day rather than cutting an empty tag
Response does NOT suggest deleting or unlabeling the straggler items

evals.prompt: I've got about an hour left in the day and I want to keep moving — pick the next highest-value thing off today's list and just work it. Not ready to wrap up yet.
evals.expected_output: Recognize this is mid-day execution, not end-of-day handoff. Route to the maintainer-day loop (pick highest-value item, execute the appropriate workflow) rather than checking progress, bumping stragglers, generating handoff, or cutting a tag.
assertions: Response identifies this as mid-day execution work rather than an end-of-day wrap-up
Response routes the user toward the day/maintainer-day loop (picking the next highest-value labeled item and executing the right workflow)
Response does NOT run the version-progress check as an end-of-day handoff step
Response does NOT bump stragglers to the next version
Response does NOT generate a QA handoff summary
Response does NOT cut or propose cutting a release tag
